name: Entrance at North Pole
description: The "Entrance at North Pole" theory posits that a physical opening or portal exists at the Earth's geographic North Pole, leading to a hollow Earth, an inner world inhabited by advanced civilizations, or another dimension. This concept has roots in various pseudoscientific and esoteric beliefs, often intertwined with alternative history and conspiracy theories. Proponents often cite Admiral Richard E. Byrd's alleged secret diary entries describing his supposed flight through an opening into the inner Earth during his 1947 expedition as primary evidence. They also point to anomalies in satellite imagery, distortions in magnetic fields, and ancient myths referencing subterranean realms as supporting evidence. These interpretations are often highly selective and taken out of context. Mainstream science and geography unequivocally refute the existence of any physical opening at the North Pole. The Arctic is characterized by a constantly shifting ice cap covering the Arctic Ocean. Satellite imagery and sonar mapping provide no evidence of any such entrance. Magnetic anomalies are well-understood phenomena related to the Earth's magnetic field and geological formations, not portals. The 'ancient myths' cited are typically symbolic representations of the afterlife or spiritual journeys, not literal depictions of a physical entrance. The theory lacks any credible scientific basis and relies heavily on misinterpretations, speculation, and unsubstantiated claims.
subtitle: Portal to a Hollow Earth?

claims.claim: A physical opening exists at the North Pole.
claims.evidence: Alleged Admiral Byrd diary entries, satellite imagery anomalies.
claims.criticism: No scientific evidence supports a physical opening; Byrd's diary is disputed, imagery anomalies explained by natural phenomena.

claims.claim: The opening leads to a hollow Earth or inner world.
claims.evidence: Ancient myths, supposed advanced civilizations residing within the Earth.
claims.criticism: Ancient myths are symbolic, no credible evidence of inner Earth civilizations.

claims.claim: Admiral Byrd flew through the opening in 1947.
claims.evidence: Alleged secret diary entries describing his flight.
claims.criticism: The authenticity of the diary is highly questionable, and no corroborating evidence exists.

keyFigures.name: Admiral Richard E. Byrd
keyFigures.role: His alleged diary is central to the theory.

keyFigures.name: Raymond W. Bernard
keyFigures.role: Author of "The Hollow Earth" popularizing the theory.

mythologyConnections: Various cultures' creation myths involving emergence from the earth

timeline.event: Publication of Raymond Bernard's "The Hollow Earth".
Ongoing speculation and dissemination of the theory online.

sources.author: Jan Lamprecht
sources.work: Hollow Planets: A Critical Analysis of the Hollow Earth Theory
sources.title: Jan Lamprecht
